paper.title_en: A new upper limit on the direct 3α decay of the Hoyle state
paper.abstract_en: The Hoyle state in 12C is pivotal in stellar nucleosynthesis as the gateway for carbon synthesis during the triple-α process. While its dominant decay proceeds sequentially via the ground state of 8Be, the possibility of a direct three-α decay (DD) channel remains a critical probe of its underlying α-cluster structure and has far-reaching astrophysical implications. Here we report a new high-precision measurement of the DD branching ratio utilizing an optimized array of eight double-sided silicon strip detectors and advanced kinematic reconstruction techniques, yielding over 2.2 × 105 fully reconstructed Hoyle state events, the highest statistics so far. The data analysis employs both the folded Dalitz plot projections and normalized energy difference methods, supplemented by Bayesian soft-assignment classification, to disentangle DD events from the overwhelming sequential decay background. We determine an upper limit of 0.018% (0.0026%) for the DDϕ (DDE) mode at the 2σ confidence level and a realistic branching ratio of approximately 0.002%−0.0016%+0.0025% for DDϕ using Bayesian inference. These results provide the lowest experimental upper limits to date, closer to the recent theoretical predictions, and reinforce the dominance of the sequential decay.
